role_title: Voice of the Customer Program Manager
secondary_archetype: Voice of the Customer / Insights Program Manager

level_signals: Compensation range $127K–$269K base (wide band; localized for remote)
Cross-functional influence across Product, Engineering, Sales, Support, Research, Marketing
Described as building something 'at company scale'

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience building and running a VOC program, as a PM or Manager, with a strong POV on what good looks like
A background in a customer-facing function (Support, Success, or Sales – Support preferred)
Demonstrated ownership mentality — a track record of identifying problems, driving solutions, and ruthlessly prioritizing when capacity is limited
Fluency in AI workflows and demonstrated ability to build lightweight, scalable processes quickly

Strong analytical and communication skills, with a demonstrated ability to translate data into compelling customer narratives, influence cross-functional stakeholders, and drive action across a range of audiences

scope.surface: Company-wide Voice of the Customer (VOC) program, sitting within the Product Support org
primary_metrics_inferred: Speed of insight delivery to Product and Engineering
Wins returned to Support and Sales
core_responsibilities: Own the rhythm of VOC reporting and define mechanisms that feed into the program, including launch insight reporting
Surface and prioritize the biggest customer pain points and align with Product and Engineering on roadmap prioritization
Monitor and report on customer sentiment from pre through post-product launches
Set shared, data-backed goals with Product and Engineering partners and monitor progress
Work directly with Sales to bring customer problem statements into the VOC program
Integrate signals from NPS, Research, Sales, and community alongside Support data into a unified narrative
Strategically implement AI workflows to build scalable processes that generate and deliver insights faster
Communicate customer insights across a range of audiences, from frontline support to Product leadership

green_flags: Fully remote-US eligible, not limited to hub cities
Clear program ownership and mandate described ('build something that matters at company scale')
Encourages applications even if past experience doesn't align perfectly
Equity and comprehensive benefits package explicitly described
AI workflow fluency explicitly valued, signaling forward-thinking team
red_flags: Compensation range is extremely wide ($127K–$269K), making it difficult to gauge target level without recruiter conversation
Remote pay is localized at 80–100% of range, which could significantly reduce compensation depending on location
No years of experience minimum stated; seniority expectations are ambiguous given the wide comp band

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and scope of responsibilities; not explicitly stated)
scope.business_model (inferred from Figma's known product and market)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not directly listed in JD)
ambiguities: Customer-facing background requirement lists 'Support, Success, or Sales – Support preferred': Support preferred is an added qualifier that could be read as hard (preferred over others) or as making Success/Sales also acceptable; classified as hard must-have with the note that the preference signals Support is most valued.

The compensation range ($127K–$269K) is very wide and the JD notes it applies to SF/NY hub offices, with remote roles localized at 80–100% of range. Actual target band for a given candidate is unclear without recruiter input.
